output.thread_descriptor: The question of Crenshinibon's location
output.evidence_quote: "Where is it?" "Al Dimeneira threw it."

output.thread_descriptor: Crenshinibon's arrival and resting place in the northern mountains
output.evidence_quote: Far up in the northern mountains of the Forgotten Realms the crystal shard... settled into the snow of a bowl-shaped dell.

output.thread_descriptor: The wizards' entry into Icewind Dale and arrival at Ten-Towns
output.evidence_quote: When the wizards' caravan from the Hosttower of the Arcane saw the snow-capped peak of Kelvin's Cairn rising from the flat horizon, they were more than a little relieved.

output.thread_descriptor: Kessell's transformation from intimidated apprentice to ambitious would-be wizard lord
output.evidence_quote: Gradually, the confidence that had put him in this dangerous position began to grow again within him. He was the boss now

output.thread_descriptor: Kessell's transition from apprentice to wizard after murdering his master
output.evidence_quote: Akar Kessell, this day forward known as Kessell the Red, is now appointed to the Wizard's Guild of Luskan!

output.thread_descriptor: Kessell's delusion of becoming a powerful wizard
output.evidence_quote: Kessell looked down at his singed fingertip and wondered what he had done wrong. But he wasn't overly dismayed. His own blackened nail was the strongest effect he had ever gotten from that particular spell.

output.thread_descriptor: Regis's exile and procrastination
output.evidence_quote: Regis meant to do a lot of things.

output.thread_descriptor: The barbarian invasion of Ten-Towns
output.evidence_quote: And Drizzt had heard the battle drums.

output.thread_descriptor: Regis's contemplation of his haven in Ten-Towns and his connection to Pasha Pook
output.evidence_quote: Regis pulled the ruby pendant out from under his waistcoat and stared at the wondrous gem he had appropriated from his former master

output.thread_descriptor: Drizzt's survival and bond with Bruenor in the tundra
output.evidence_quote: Smiling in relief, and also at the mannerisms of the everamazing dwarf, Drizzt managed to wriggle out from under the monster

output.thread_descriptor: Barbarian council to unite tribes and conquer Ten-Towns
output.evidence_quote: Beorg's plan called for the total conquest of Ten-Towns, enslaving the surviving fishermen and living well off of the wealth they harvested from the lakes.

output.thread_descriptor: Heafstaag's march to the tribal encampment at Hengorot
output.evidence_quote: When the lights of the campfires came into view, the column quickened its pace.

output.thread_descriptor: Kessell's abandonment and struggle for survival in the Spine of the World
output.evidence_quote: They had dropped him in the Spine of the World. They had left him to die.

output.thread_descriptor: Kessell's transformation from dying weakling to the Tyrant of Icewind Dale
output.evidence_quote: He would become the Akar Kessell of his own fantasies, and all would bow before him.

output.thread_descriptor: Kessell's transformation from survivalist to powerful wizard and the awakening of his purpose with Crenshinibon
output.evidence_quote: Kessell felt strangely changed that morning. The night before, he had been concerned only with the quantity of his life... But now he pondered the quality of his life.

output.thread_descriptor: Kessell's transformation from timid peasant to power-wielding conqueror under Crenshinibon's influence
output.evidence_quote: When he awakened the next morning, he remembered the dream and found that he liked the idea.

output.thread_descriptor: Kessell's assertion of dominance over the goblin chieftain
output.evidence_quote: The chieftain's reply was torn from within by a power that it could not resist. "Master."

output.thread_descriptor: Bruenor's reluctant admission of care for Ten-Towns and his found family
output.evidence_quote: he had come to care for the people who had settled on the banks of the three lakes

output.thread_descriptor: formation of the Ten-Towns defensive alliance against the barbarian horde
output.evidence_quote: the barbarian tribes are gathering for a united attack on Ten-Towns! [...] Unless we form an alliance, the horde will overrun our communities one by one

output.thread_descriptor: The barbarian invasion of Ten-Towns
output.evidence_quote: They started at dawn, charging across the tundra like an angry whirlwind

output.thread_descriptor: The barbarian horde's approach and impending attack on Ten-Towns
output.evidence_quote: You have gotten your wish," Drizzt said with certainty.

output.thread_descriptor: Drizzt's reconnaissance of the barbarian attack plans
output.evidence_quote: Drizzt watched closely as deBernezan described this second division of the barbarian forces on his map.

output.thread_descriptor: Guenhwyvar's passage from the astral plane to the material plane and subsequent return to Drizzt's side
output.evidence_quote: The great cat's spirit rushed down the long, darkened corridor that marked the void between the planes, seeking the solitary speck of light that was its life on the material plane.

output.thread_descriptor: Drizzt's moral awakening and bond with Guenhwyvar
output.evidence_quote: he couldn't label it, for he had never come across a word in the drow language that spoke of such a trait, but to the surfacedwellers that later came to know Drizzt, it was called conscience.

output.thread_descriptor: Barbarian attack on Termalaine and the Ten-Towns ambush
output.evidence_quote: Torches set the first buildings of the city ablaze as Haalfdane's wild army rushed in.

output.thread_descriptor: The Battle of Bryn Shander
output.evidence_quote: the Tribe of the Elk was outnumbered and out-flanked, and barbarian blood ran freer than the blood of their foes.

output.thread_descriptor: Bruenor's reluctance and regret in killing the young barbarian standard-bearer
output.evidence_quote: the dwarf found himself truly lamenting having to kill one so young and unusual, and his pity caused him to hesitate slightly as the two joined battle

output.thread_descriptor: Drizzt's duel with Heafstaag during the barbarian invasion
output.evidence_quote: Heafstaag's rage doubled when he recognized his newest opponent as a drow elf.

output.thread_descriptor: The battle of Bryn Shander and the defeat of the barbarian horde
output.evidence_quote: Less than fifty escaped with their lives.

output.thread_descriptor: Bruenor's recognition of Regis's heroism after the battle
output.evidence_quote: Bruenor snorted in amusement as the procession passed. "There's more to that tale than what's been told, I'll wager!"

output.thread_descriptor: The dissolution of the Ten-Towns alliance after the battle
output.evidence_quote: And so it went that the alliance of the people of Ten-Towns lasted only as long as their common enemy.

output.thread_descriptor: Regis's transition from reluctant spokesman to comfortable, profit-seeking idler
output.evidence_quote: It seemed that he could "persuade" almost anyone of almost anything these days.

output.thread_descriptor: Wulfgar's character growth from angry captive to grateful craftsman under Bruenor's tutelage
output.evidence_quote: He understood now, to his own amazement, that he had become a better man under the tutelage of Bruenor Battlehammer

output.thread_descriptor: The compelled entry of Torga and Grock into Cryshal-Tirith to face Akar Kessell
output.evidence_quote: They entered Cryshal-Tirith and stood before Akar Kessell.

output.thread_descriptor: Kessell's transformation from content wizard to aspiring conqueror
output.evidence_quote: Kessell had been quite content with his life in Cryshal-Tirith... But Crenshinibon was not contented.

output.thread_descriptor: Errtu's entry into the material plane via a reckless summoning
output.evidence_quote: Errtu stepped through. The demon didn't pause at all to consider the terrified young human that had foolishly called its name.

output.thread_descriptor: Errtu's pursuit of Crenshinibon on the material plane
output.evidence_quote: Errtu felt the familiar sensation of power more distinctly on the material plane and had little trouble discerning the direction of the emanations.

output.thread_descriptor: Errtu's conversion from enemy to ally in Kessell's army
output.evidence_quote: He had his general.

output.thread_descriptor: Bruenor's access to his secret treasure chamber under Kelvin's Cairn
output.evidence_quote: He entered the room, putting his torch in before him to burn away the hanging fringes of many cobwebs.

output.thread_descriptor: Bruenor's decision to craft his masterpiece weapon
output.evidence_quote: Bruenor's day had come.

output.thread_descriptor: Bruenor's forging of the two-headed hammer
output.evidence_quote: he knew that he would meet his demanding schedule. He would attach the adamantite handle the next night and all would be ready for the enchantment under the full moon on the night of the summer solstice.

output.thread_descriptor: Bruenor's enchantment of the mithril warhammer
output.evidence_quote: Bruenor felt his very strength being sucked from him as he uttered the words of power

output.thread_descriptor: Wulfgar's role-shift from defiant pride to acknowledgment of his feelings about Bruenor
output.evidence_quote: He didn't stop to think of why he was so mad at Catti-brie. He knew that if he did, he would find, as usual, that he was angry because her observations hit the mark.

output.thread_descriptor: Bruenor's completion of the warhammer and request for Drizzt to train Wulfgar
output.evidence_quote: Entranced by the apparent perfection of his work, Bruenor understood the emptiness his father had spoken of.

output.thread_descriptor: Wulfgar's emotional and role shift regarding his indenture and Bruenor's regard
output.evidence_quote: He told himself at once that he would pray for many nights to the gods of fate for delivering this prize unto him.

output.thread_descriptor: Kessell's invasion of the region begins with sending Biggrin's scouting group
output.evidence_quote: You are the first group to go into the area, and secrecy is the key to our victory!

output.thread_descriptor: Kessell's invasion of Ten-Towns
output.evidence_quote: Heafstaag's body went rigid. 'To Akar Kessell!' he responded, to his own repulsion.

output.thread_descriptor: Wulfgar's introduction to Drizzt as his mentor
output.evidence_quote: Wulfgar's eyes widened in horror and disgust. 'A dark elf!' he cried incredulously.

output.thread_descriptor: Wulfgar's return to the open tundra of Icewind Dale after five years
output.evidence_quote: For the first time in nearly five years, Wulfgar looked out beyond the borders of Ten-Towns to the open stretch of Icewind Dale that spread wide before him.

output.thread_descriptor: Drizzt training Wulfgar in combat and discipline
output.evidence_quote: Drizzt could see the subtle change taking place as the barbarian stopped reacting after the fact to the scimitars' deft cuts and thrusts and began recognizing his own vulnerable areas and anticipating the next attack.

output.thread_descriptor: Wulfgar's understanding of true kingship under Drizzt's guidance
output.evidence_quote: "He is a savage fighter," Drizzt corrected.

output.thread_descriptor: Biggrin's struggle to maintain control over the verbeeg troop
output.evidence_quote: Biggrin had given them back the promise of action, though the giant leader's doubts about its decision were far from dispelled by the lusty enthusiasm of the soldiers.

output.thread_descriptor: The verbeeg patrol discovering the dwarven miners on Kelvin's Cairn
output.evidence_quote: So it happened that the first patrol of verbeeg to leave the lair that night soon spotted the flames of a campfire and heard the distinctive dialect of the hated dwarves.

output.thread_descriptor: Drizzt and Wulfgar's deepening understanding and mutual reflection on identity and morality
output.evidence_quote: Perhaps we two are not so different.

output.thread_descriptor: Verbeeg attack on the dwarf camp near Kelvin's Cairn
output.evidence_quote: The verbeeg came bounding around some boulders just a few yards from their camp and closed in on them.

output.thread_descriptor: The war against Ten-Towns
output.evidence_quote: He had no way of knowing that the first blows of the war had already been struck.
output.abstain_reason: Scene is purely expository; no thread open/close

output.thread_descriptor: Wulfgar's internal conflict about the people of Ten-Towns
output.evidence_quote: Fearless in any battle, Wulfgar was truly frightened of facing those people.

output.thread_descriptor: Drizzt's tracking and discovery of the verbeeg lair
output.evidence_quote: Drizzt, too, started away, tracing the trail of the three giants westward across the face of Kelvin's Cairn. Eventually, he heard the baritone voices of giants, and shortly thereafter he saw the hastily constructed wooden doors that marked their lair

output.thread_descriptor: The dwarves' response to the giant invasion
output.evidence_quote: We'll watch over the lair.

output.thread_descriptor: Bruenor's ambush at Daledrop pass
output.evidence_quote: There was only one pass that afforded easy access down into the rocky valley: Daledrop

output.thread_descriptor: The attack on the verbeeg patrol
output.evidence_quote: The lair will be none the wiser if these giants never return

output.thread_descriptor: Wulfgar's acceptance of Guenhwyvar as an ally
output.evidence_quote: Drizzt motioned to Wulfgar to stand ready, then sent the great cat into action.

output.thread_descriptor: The Battle of Daledrop ambush on the verbeeg war party
output.evidence_quote: The Battle of Daledrop was over in three minutes.

output.thread_descriptor: Wulfgar's acceptance of his bond with Drizzt
output.evidence_quote: He would follow Drizzt into any adventure, no matter how improbable their chances of surviving. And he was beginning to admit to himself that he enjoyed gambling alongside the dark elf.

output.thread_descriptor: The raid on the verbeeg lair
output.evidence_quote: Wulfgar burst through the door and pounced at the nearest target.

error: Could not extract JSON from response:

output.thread_descriptor: The revelation of Akar Kessell's power over the giants
output.evidence_quote: I serve the master! Glory is to die for Akar Kessell!

output.thread_descriptor: The question of the empty giant room and the hidden exit in the verbeeg lair
output.evidence_quote: The room was empty. A door previously invisible to Drizzt from his vantage point at the hearth stood ajar.

output.thread_descriptor: Biggrin's attempt to redeem itself in Kessell's eyes by killing the intruders
output.evidence_quote: Biggrin's only chance was to take care of the two intruding warriors and hope that their heads would appease its unmerciful boss.

output.thread_descriptor: The battle with Biggrin, giant chieftain of the verbeeg lair
output.evidence_quote: Biggrin fell dead without another word.

output.thread_descriptor: Drizzt's exploration of the mysterious tunnel in the giant lair
output.evidence_quote: the dinner table wouldn't be his first stop. The giant they had spoken with earlier had been evasive when asked about what lay down the one tunnel they had yet to explore.

output.thread_descriptor: Guenhwyvar's herding and killing of the fleeing verbeeg
output.evidence_quote: the cat was satisfied that the mission was complete

output.thread_descriptor: The mystery of the scrying mirror and the mage's identity
output.evidence_quote: Suddenly a pinkish mist began to swirl within the mirror, giving the appearance of a three-dimensional space trapped within the flatness of the glass.

output.thread_descriptor: The question of the orc's knowledge about the alliance of giants and orcs
output.evidence_quote: Go for Regis... The halfling can make the orc tell us everything we want to know.

output.thread_descriptor: Akar Kessell's invasion of Ten-Towns
output.evidence_quote: All of the orc and goblin tribes and verbeeg clans of this region of the Spine of the World are banding together under a sorcerer named Akar Kessell,

output.thread_descriptor: Regis's attempt to persuade the council of the invasion threat
output.evidence_quote: Regis looked about, flustered and unable to even answer the spokesman's accusations.

output.thread_descriptor: Wulfgar's transition from indentured servant to free man
output.evidence_quote: I am Wulfgar! No more a boy but a man! A free man!

output.thread_descriptor: The defense of Ten-Towns against Akar Kessell's invasion
output.evidence_quote: the greater good of the region had fallen beneath the lesser personal gains of stubborn pride

output.thread_descriptor: Regis's shift from hope to callous resignation regarding Ten-Towns' defense
output.evidence_quote: callousness was his only protection against such a helpless situation

output.thread_descriptor: Kessell's invasion of Ten-Towns begins
output.evidence_quote: the vast army of Akar Kessell pulled up its camp and began its long march toward Ten-Towns.

output.thread_descriptor: Evermelt hot spring and the legendary white dragon's lair
output.evidence_quote: At the base of the great glacier, hidden off in a small dell where one of the ice spurs wound through broken rifts and boulders, was a place the barbarians called Evermelt.

output.thread_descriptor: Wulfgar's descent through Evermelt to the dragon's lair
output.evidence_quote: The air above the pool was veiled in steam, and chunks of floating ice drifted through the misty waters like meandering ghost ships.

output.thread_descriptor: The dragon Icingdeath's defeat in its ice lair
output.evidence_quote: Icingdeath exploded from its ice prison, shaking the cavern

output.thread_descriptor: Drizzt's acquisition of the scimitar
output.evidence_quote: A scimitar. Its curving blade was of silver, and diamond-edged. Drizzt raised it before him, marveling at its lightness and perfect balance.

output.thread_descriptor: Wulfgar and Drizzt's escape from Icingdeath's lair
output.evidence_quote: managed to scramble onto the ice before the westering sun dipped below the horizon

output.thread_descriptor: Wulfgar's return to the Tribe of the Elk with a changed worldview and his impending confrontation with Heafstaag
output.evidence_quote: He had viewed battle differently then, as a glorious test of a warrior. 'Innocent savagery,' he mumbled, ... his perceptions had undergone a considerable change.

output.thread_descriptor: Wulfgar's challenge to Heafstaag for leadership of the Tribe of the Elk
output.evidence_quote: I am Wulfgar, Dragon's bane!

output.thread_descriptor: Wulfgar's challenge for the kingship of the Tribe of the Elk
output.evidence_quote: Wulfgar wanted more from this battle than to simply win the leadership of his tribe; he wanted to leave a lasting impression on all of the witnesses.

output.thread_descriptor: The burning of Caer-Konig and Caer-Dineval (the assault on Ten-Towns)
output.evidence_quote: Caer-Konig and Caer-Dineval were burning.

output.thread_descriptor: Kessell's invasion of Ten-Towns
output.evidence_quote: Like an angry swarm of bees, Kessell's foul army swept right around the northern bend of Lac Dinneshere and roared down Icewind Pass.

output.thread_descriptor: Agorwal's sacrifice to save the refugees of Termalaine
output.evidence_quote: His contented smile was sincere as he spun around and charged back down the grade to join his victorious troops in their finest moment.

output.thread_descriptor: The dwarves' defense of Mithril Hall valley against Kessell's army
output.evidence_quote: the only force that actually battled Kessell's army that day and survived were the dwarves

output.thread_descriptor: The invasion's disruption of Ten-Towns' status quo and the rivalries between them
output.evidence_quote: Their disputes with Kemp's people seemed petty indeed against the weight of the disaster that had befallen the towns.

output.thread_descriptor: The siege of Bryn Shander by Kessell's army
output.evidence_quote: the main body of the army veered away from Bryn Shander and moved down the swath of open ground between the principle city and Targos, thus cutting any possibility of the two powerful cities linking their forces.

output.thread_descriptor: The raising of Cryshal-Tirith by Akar Kessell
output.evidence_quote: The tower released its hold on the sun as the ritual was completed... Akar Kessell, master of goblins and giants, had come.

output.thread_descriptor: Drizzt's resolve to assassinate Akar Kessell
output.evidence_quote: The drow determined at that moment that the only chance for survival for the people of Ten-Towns lay in the death of Akar Kessell.

output.thread_descriptor: The transformation of the eastern refugees from despair to vengeful warriors after news of Bryn Shander's survival
output.evidence_quote: Suddenly, Brent's optimism took on credibility. The miserable band of homeless victims assumed an angry posture bent on vengeance.

output.thread_descriptor: Kessell's devastating display of power and destruction of Targos
output.evidence_quote: The wrath of Akar Kessell roared into the proud city, cutting a swath of instant devastation.

output.thread_descriptor: Kemp's mobilization of Targos's fleet in response to the goblin invasion
output.evidence_quote: Kemp converted his despair into constructive anger.

output.thread_descriptor: The identity and true name of the demon Errtu
output.evidence_quote: Not only this type of demon, but this particular creature itself, was known to Drizzt. ... 'Errtu,' he whispered ... Drizzt knew the demon's true name. It would come to his call.

output.thread_descriptor: Drizzt's preparation and mental resolve to confront Errtu and extract information about Kessell's weakness
output.evidence_quote: He had no intentions of fighting. Yet he had to try something against the wizard who threatened the towns. His goal now was to gain some knowledge that might reveal a weakness in the wizard, and his method was deception and disguise

output.thread_descriptor: Drizzt Do'Urden's transition from deceptive spy to open ranger of Mielikki
output.evidence_quote: I am Drizzt Do'Urden... A ranger humbly serving Gwaeron Windstrom, hero of the goddess Mielikki.

output.thread_descriptor: Wulfgar's assertion of authority and decisive march against Kessell
output.evidence_quote: I shall leave tonight!

output.thread_descriptor: Kessell's war of conquest in Ten-Towns
output.evidence_quote: The meekest of animals will fight bravely when it is backed against a wall

output.thread_descriptor: Bruenor's dwarves digging shafts to attack Kessell's army from below
output.evidence_quote: they dug ten separate upward shafts, stopping just shy of the surface

output.thread_descriptor: The question of whether Regis can deceive Kessell to delay the siege
output.evidence_quote: I have some information that I am sure you will find valuable

output.thread_descriptor: Drizzt's need to keep Guenhwyvar in the material plane despite the cat's suffering
output.evidence_quote: Drizzt needed the cat's eyes.

output.thread_descriptor: Regis's deception of Kessell regarding the Orcs of the Severed Tongue
output.evidence_quote: Regis drew the wizard fully into the deception... watching the wizard's widening eyes with complete satisfaction.

output.thread_descriptor: Kessell's siege of Ten-Towns
output.evidence_quote: a second group of monsters flowed out from the main force and headed in the direction of Bremen

output.thread_descriptor: Drizzt's infiltration of Cryshal-Tirith
output.evidence_quote: The ogres never even noticed the two shadows enter the tower behind them.

output.thread_descriptor: Drizzt's entry into Cryshal-Tirith and discovery of the trolls
output.evidence_quote: Drizzt peered around at the dark forms in the room and the deeper shadows that they created. ... This was the place where Akar Kessell housed his trolls.

output.thread_descriptor: The dwarves' raid on Kessell's monster camp
output.evidence_quote: The dwarves of Mithril Hall completed the first of their secret exits shortly after sunset.

output.thread_descriptor: The defeat of Akar Kessell and the fall of Cryshal-Tirith
output.evidence_quote: I have defeated you, Akar Kessell. Your short reign as self-proclaimed ruler of Ten-Towns is ended.

output.thread_descriptor: The final Battle of Icewind Dale
output.evidence_quote: In a whirlwind rush of chaos and surprise, the final Battle of Icewind Dale had begun.

output.thread_descriptor: The destruction of Cryshal-Tirith and the fate of Regis and Guenhwyvar
output.evidence_quote: Blocks of crystalline rubble began falling all about as the tower shuddered in its final death throes.

output.thread_descriptor: Drizzt's arrival on Kelvin's Cairn after escaping Cryshal-Tirith
output.evidence_quote: He dropped into a deep pile of snow and knew that he was not dreaming.

output.thread_descriptor: The battle for Bryn Shander and the collapse of goblin resistance
output.evidence_quote: The soldiers of Bryn Shander and Easthaven... charged down the sloping hill and drove hard into the confused ranks of goblins.

output.thread_descriptor: Defeat of Akar Kessell
output.evidence_quote: Akar Kessell felt weak again for the first time in years. Not the Tyrant of Icewind Dale, but the bumbling apprentice who had murdered his teacher.

output.thread_descriptor: The unraveling of the monster army's cohesion after Kessell's fall
output.evidence_quote: The monster army had seen their god fall again. The thread that had incited their momentum quickly began to unravel.

output.thread_descriptor: The Lac Dinneshere army's landing and advance to the Eastway
output.evidence_quote: They leaped from the boats and splashed ashore, rushing into the battle with a fearless frenzy that drove their opponents away.

output.thread_descriptor: The assault on Bryn Shander after Termalaine is cleared
output.evidence_quote: Kemp ran to the highest spot he could find, his face flushed with a vengeful desire. 'Break the line, boys!' he shouted to his men.

output.thread_descriptor: The battle for Bryn Shander
output.evidence_quote: The battle raged for the remainder of the morning.

output.thread_descriptor: The allied charge against the goblin army
output.evidence_quote: As one killing wave the human and dwarven allies charged down the hill.

output.thread_descriptor: Drizzt's descent from Kelvin's Cairn after escaping Cryshal-Tirith
output.evidence_quote: He resolved to plod on, making for the western face which offered the best protection against the wind.

output.thread_descriptor: The goblin invasion of Ten-Towns
output.evidence_quote: the last of the monsters fell dead to the blood-stained field

output.thread_descriptor: Drizzt's descent from Kelvin's Cairn and approach to Cryshal-Tirith's rubble
output.evidence_quote: He finished his descent from Kelvin's Cairn and headed down Bremen's Run-toward the rubble.

output.thread_descriptor: The alliance between the barbarians and Ten-Towns
output.evidence_quote: I request an invitation to your council, Cassius. There is much that our people can offer to each other in this harsh time.

output.thread_descriptor: The fate of Regis after the collapse of Cryshal-Tirith
output.evidence_quote: He reached in and pulled out the golden chain with the ruby pendent, and his fears were confirmed.

output.thread_descriptor: Drizzt's promise to accompany Bruenor to Mithril Hall in the spring
output.evidence_quote: In the spring. I shall see you to your home in the spring!

output.thread_descriptor: Bruenor's mercy toward the barbarian boy Wulfgar
output.evidence_quote: laid it down gently instead

output.thread_descriptor: Kessell's shift from complacent wizard to aspiring conqueror of Ten-Towns
output.evidence_quote: the wizard decided, though, that he would certainly consider the possibilities of lordship over Ten-Towns that had opened wide before him

output.thread_descriptor: Wulfgar's journey to Evermelt to slay Icingdeath
output.evidence_quote: Toward the lair of Ingeloakastimizilian, the white dragon the barbarians simply called 'Icingdeath.'

output.thread_descriptor: The post-war reconstruction and formalization of the alliance between Ten-Towns and barbarians
output.evidence_quote: When all was finally settled, the barbarians were given the cities of Bremen and Caer-Konig to rebuild

output.thread_descriptor: Drizzt's acceptance of his outcast status as he prepares to seek Mithril Hall
output.evidence_quote: But the drow accepted the shunning with his characteristic stoicism.

output.thread_descriptor: Wulfgar's decision to leave with the companions
output.evidence_quote: You go because you choose the road!

output.thread_descriptor: The quest to find Mithril Hall
output.evidence_quote: One fine morning in early spring, the time finally came for Drizzt and Bruenor to leave.

output.thread_descriptor: The identity of the mysterious man from Luskan
output.evidence_quote: He recognized the man at once, though he hadn't seen him in years, by the emerald-encrusted dagger hilt protruding from the sheath on his hip.

output.thread_descriptor: The companions' journey from Bremen into the tundra toward Mithril Hall
output.evidence_quote: The three companions left Bremen before dawn the next day.
